title: Activation of Simvastatin
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d02r5","text":"Activation of simvastatin lactone to active simvastatin acid by hydrolysis of lactone ring with NaOH.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: For every 8.4 mg of simvastatin sodium salt, dissolve in 0.2 mL of 100% ethanol.
Add 30 uL of 1N NaOH for every 8.4 mg of simvastatin sodium salt.
Heat the solution for at 50 °C for 2 hours.
Neutralize the solution to pH 7.2 with 1N HCl.
Bring to a volume of 1 mL per 8.4 mg of simvastatin dissolved in Step 1 with distilled H2O.
Dilute 1:1 with DMSO to yield a 10mM solution with 50% DMSO, and 10% EtOH in H2O.
creator: Dennis Juarez
